explanation: TRUE POSITIVE. The vulnerability is confirmed through full data flow tracing and mathematical proof. In `_claimRewardExtra` (lines 127-128), extra rewards are prorated as `storedPendingRewardExtra[account] * record.extraRewardWeight / l.extraRewardWeight[account]`. The `_unstake` function (lines 54-87) has no expiry check, so a still-locked record can be withdrawn at any time, unconditionally decrementing `l.extraRewardWeight[account]` at line 82. Because `_claimRewardExtra` returns 0 for locked records (line 120-121) without touching `storedPendingRewardExtra`, the accumulated balance is left intact while the denominator shrinks. When an expired record is subsequently unstaked, the collapsed denominator makes the ratio equal to 1.0 (W_A/W_A), transferring 100% of the stored extra rewards to the attacker—including the portion attributable to the early-withdrawn locked stake. No special privileges, reentrancy, or oracle manipulation are required; only normal user staking operations in a chosen sequence. The `LiquidityManagerStorage.StakeRecord` struct has no per-record claimed-reward field that could prevent this double-attribution. The attack is reliable and feasible over a multi-day window between the expiry of the shorter and longer stakes.
